mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the vLLM command-line interface using pip to manage deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.19.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a GKE cluster with the required version and node count for running vLLM.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the Ready state.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the vLLM application using Helm to manage the deployment.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.0

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in the vllm namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for the vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment vllm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace vllm

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Configure horizontal pod autoscaler.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description:
Be aware of potential costs associated with running the GKE cluster and vLLM resources.

```bash

gcloud compute instances list

```

> Check running instances and their costs.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data related to the deployment.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the vLLM Helm release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Ensure you back up any necessary data before upgrading. Drain and cordon nodes if needed, then perform the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> Upgrade to the latest version of vLLM.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or resources allocated to the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and correct the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The request to the Kubernetes API server timed out.

**Fix:**
Check the network connectivity and ensure the API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.18.0, ensure to update your model configurations to align with the new standards.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n vllm' showing all pods in the Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and cloud CLI installed before proceeding.
